summary:
The agent evaluates each option against published data showing common SSRI-associated adverse events such as vivid dreaming, anorgasmia, and nausea. It concludes that sialorrhea lacks documentation among recognized escitalopram or SSRI side effects due to absence in clinical reports about increased salivation; thus, it remains unexplained compared to other choices.

The agent correctly identifies that while escitalopram commonly causes vivid dreaming, anorgasmia, and nausea, sialorrhea has no association with its pharmacological profile based on existing clinical evidence.
